title: Copy List with Random Pointer
problem_statement: Create a deep copy of a linked list where each node has a next pointer and a random pointer.

examples.explanation: The copied list should have the same values and random connections, but new nodes.

constraints: Node.random is null or points to a node in the list.
intuition: Copy nodes with a hash map or interleave copied nodes to preserve random pointers.

approaches.name: Interleave and separate
approaches.description: Clone each node and insert it after the original, assign random pointers using the interleaved structure, then separate the copied list.
approaches.why_not: A hashmap solution uses O(n) extra space even though an in-place approach exists.

interview_qa.a: It allows assigning random pointers to copied nodes without extra mapping structures.

interview_qa.q: How do you restore the original list?
interview_qa.a: Separate the combined list by rewiring next pointers for original and copied nodes.
